seoTitle: Old Toongabbie vs Olney — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest

ogTitle: Old Toongabbie vs Olney: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Old Toongabbie and Olney.
heading: Old Toongabbie vs Olney — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Old Toongabbie, New South Wales and Olney, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Olney has a lower overall climate risk score (40/100) compared to Old Toongabbie (42/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Old Toongabbie or Olney safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Old Toongabbie has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Olney scores 27/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Old Toongabbie scores 49/100 for bushfire risk. Olney scores 32/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Old Toongabbie or Olney?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Olney has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
